# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-06-05

Uber exhausted its 2026 AI coding tool budget in four months and is now rate-limiting Claude Code usage to manage costs.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Uber Caps Usage of AI Tools Like Claude Code to Manage Costs
   Concrete cost signal for AI coding tool spend — relevant to anyone budgeting for these tools.

2. Gemma 4 12B: A unified, encoder-free multimodal model
   New open multimodal model at a size runnable locally — directly impacts local model users.

3. Elixir v1.20: Now a gradually typed language
   Major language release with a fundamental new feature — significant in the broader PL landscape.

4. I built a vulnerable app and spent $1,500 seeing if LLMs could hack it
   Reproducible security research with real cost data on LLM-based vulnerability scanning.

5. Trace any Vercel request from the CLI
   Practical debugging tool for Vercel users — ties into observability workflows.
